canon.name: Afrobeat
canon.shortDescription: West African popular music combining groove, horns, extended forms and political expression.

canon.name: Ambient
canon.shortDescription: Music focused on atmosphere, texture, space and sustained listening environments.

canon.name: Ancient music
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for ancient music.

canon.name: Baroque
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for baroque.

canon.name: Black Atlantic music
canon.shortDescription: Transatlantic Black musical continuum linking African diasporic histories and modern popular forms.

canon.name: Blues
canon.shortDescription: African American musical form centered on expressive vocal style, blue notes and repeated harmonic patterns.

canon.name: Call and response
canon.shortDescription: Responsive musical structure where one phrase is answered by another.

canon.name: Classical period
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for classical period.

canon.name: Club culture
canon.shortDescription: Nightlife and DJ-centered spaces where dance music scenes, taste and technology develop.

canon.name: Country
canon.shortDescription: Popular music tradition drawing on folk ballads, rural storytelling and string-band practices.

canon.name: Dance culture
canon.shortDescription: Social contexts where music is organized around dancing, bodies and shared movement.

canon.name: Dancehall
canon.shortDescription: Jamaican music culture emphasizing deejay vocals, digital riddims and sound-system performance.

canon.name: Digital music age
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for digital music age.

canon.name: Disco
canon.shortDescription: Dance music culture and production style built around steady pulse, orchestration and club playback.

canon.name: Distortion
canon.shortDescription: Sound alteration caused by saturation, clipping or nonlinear amplification.

canon.name: Drone
canon.shortDescription: Sustained or repeated pitch foundation used as a tonal reference.

canon.name: Drum machine
canon.shortDescription: Electronic instrument for sequencing and playing percussion sounds.

canon.name: Dub
canon.shortDescription: Studio-centered Jamaican practice using remixing, echo, reverb, bass and instrumental versions.

canon.name: Electronic music
canon.shortDescription: Music made with electronic sound generation, processing, sequencing or computer-based production.

canon.name: Film music
canon.shortDescription: Music composed, selected or produced to support cinematic narrative, mood and audiovisual timing.

canon.name: Folk music
canon.shortDescription: Community-based music traditions transmitted through local practice and memory.

canon.name: Funk
canon.shortDescription: Groove-driven music emphasizing syncopated bass, drums, riffs and collective rhythmic interlock.

canon.name: Game music
canon.shortDescription: Interactive and linear music created for video games, gameplay states and digital worlds.

canon.name: Gospel
canon.shortDescription: Sacred vocal tradition rooted in Christian worship, communal singing and expressive call-and-response.

canon.name: Groove
canon.shortDescription: Felt rhythmic momentum created by repeated patterns, timing and ensemble interaction.

canon.name: Harmony
canon.shortDescription: Combination and progression of pitches or chords sounding together.

canon.name: Hip hop
canon.shortDescription: Cultural and musical form built around MCing, DJing, beats, sampling and urban expression.

canon.name: House
canon.shortDescription: Electronic dance music developed around four-on-the-floor rhythms, DJs, drum machines and clubs.

canon.name: Improvisation
canon.shortDescription: Real-time musical creation within stylistic, social or formal constraints.

canon.name: Internet music culture
canon.shortDescription: Networked music circulation shaped by platforms, memes, communities and digital release practices.

canon.name: Jazz
canon.shortDescription: Improvisatory music tradition shaped by swing, blues, ensemble interaction and harmonic exploration.

canon.name: Latin music
canon.shortDescription: Broad set of Latin American and diasporic musical traditions, rhythms and popular forms.

canon.name: Loop
canon.shortDescription: Repeated audio or musical pattern used as a structural building block.

canon.name: Medieval music
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for medieval music.

canon.name: Melody
canon.shortDescription: Ordered sequence of pitches perceived as a musical line.

canon.name: Migration and music
canon.shortDescription: Musical exchange, memory and hybrid forms shaped by movement of people.

canon.name: Minimalism
canon.shortDescription: Compositional movement using repetition, gradual process and reduced musical materials.

canon.name: Modality
canon.shortDescription: Pitch organization using modes or scalar frameworks beyond major/minor tonality.

canon.name: Modernism
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for modernism.

canon.name: Multitrack recording
canon.shortDescription: Recording method that captures and mixes separate sound layers independently.

canon.name: Music video culture
canon.shortDescription: Audiovisual pop culture shaped by television, video clips, image and performance.

canon.name: Nordic folk music
canon.shortDescription: Folk traditions from Nordic regions, including dance forms, fiddle practices and local song heritage.

canon.name: Norwegian pop
canon.shortDescription: Norwegian popular music tradition shaped by local language, international pop and Nordic production contexts.

canon.name: Overtones
canon.shortDescription: Frequencies above a fundamental that shape tone color and resonance.

canon.name: Polyrhythm
canon.shortDescription: Simultaneous use of contrasting rhythmic patterns or meters.

canon.name: Pop
canon.shortDescription: Broad popular song tradition organized around hooks, mass media circulation and changing production styles.

canon.name: Post-punk
canon.shortDescription: Experimental aftermath of punk that expanded rock with electronics, dub, art-school ideas and new textures.

canon.name: Post-war music
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for post-war music.

canon.name: Protest music
canon.shortDescription: Music used to voice dissent, solidarity, critique or political mobilization.

canon.name: Punk
canon.shortDescription: Raw, fast and often anti-establishment rock movement emphasizing directness and DIY practice.

canon.name: Reggae
canon.shortDescription: Jamaican popular music marked by offbeat rhythm, bass weight, songcraft and sound-system culture.

canon.name: Renaissance music
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for renaissance music.

canon.name: Resonance
canon.shortDescription: Amplification or reinforcement of vibration in bodies, rooms or instruments.

canon.name: Reverb
canon.shortDescription: Persistence of sound reflections that creates a sense of space.

canon.name: Rhythm
canon.shortDescription: Organization of musical time through duration, pulse, accent and pattern.

canon.name: Rhythm and blues
canon.shortDescription: Post-war Black popular music emphasizing groove, vocals and dance-oriented rhythm sections.

canon.name: Ritual music
canon.shortDescription: Music used in ceremonial, sacred or repeated social practices.

canon.name: Rock
canon.shortDescription: Amplified popular music tradition built around drums, bass, guitar, vocals and album/song forms.

canon.name: Rock and roll
canon.shortDescription: 1950s popular music formed from rhythm and blues, blues, country and youth culture.

canon.name: Romanticism
canon.shortDescription: Historical music era used as a high-level timeline anchor for romanticism.

canon.name: Sampling
canon.shortDescription: Use of recorded sound fragments as material in a new production.

canon.name: Soul
canon.shortDescription: Vocal-centered popular music combining rhythm and blues, gospel intensity and secular expression.

canon.name: Sound system
canon.shortDescription: Playback and amplification culture centered on DJs, selectors and powerful speakers.

canon.name: Studio as instrument
canon.shortDescription: Production approach where recording, mixing and effects become compositional tools.

canon.name: Syncopation
canon.shortDescription: Rhythmic displacement that stresses off-beats or unexpected parts of the meter.

canon.name: Synthesizer
canon.shortDescription: Electronic instrument or system for generating and shaping sound.

canon.name: Synthpop
canon.shortDescription: Pop music centered on synthesizers, drum machines, sequencers and electronic textures.

canon.name: Techno
canon.shortDescription: Electronic dance music associated with machine rhythm, futurism and synthesized sound design.

canon.name: Timbre
canon.shortDescription: Perceived sound color that distinguishes instruments, voices and textures.

canon.name: Tonality
canon.shortDescription: Pitch organization around a central key or tonic.

canon.name: Urban music culture
canon.shortDescription: City-based musical practices shaped by density, scenes, migration and media.

canon.name: Western classical music
canon.shortDescription: Art-music tradition associated with notation, institutions and long-form composition.

canon.name: Youth culture
canon.shortDescription: Generational music practices tied to identity, style, rebellion and peer communities.
